name: Cow milk, pasteurized, vitamin A + D added, 2% fat (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Cow milk, pasteurized, vitamin A + D added, 2% fat [Milk and milk products] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered (capped at 250).

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: MANUAL, USDA.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 'Milk, reduced fat, fluid, 2% milkfat, with added vitamin A and vitamin D' (NDB 01079)
Garnsworthy et al., 'Variability of citric acid in cow milk composition', doi:10.5555/20123239825
Orotic acid in cow milk, PMID:1958838 (mean 0.618 mmol/L)
Fox & McSweeney, Dairy Chemistry and Biochemistry (free sugars, chloride, inositol, urea, carnitine)
McCance & Widdowson / UK dairy iodine reference
provenance.curation_notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is LACTOSE (~4.8 g/100g, 144.7 mM); milk protein (~3.3 g, casein+whey) is the main N source. The key correction versus the FooDB profile is adding the milk organic acids — citrate (~9.4 mM, matches textbook 8-10 mM) and orotic acid — and replacing non-milk sugars (sucrose/maltose/fructose noise) with quantified lactose plus trace free glucose/galactose. Cholesterol is genuine (animal food) and kept; toxic/ultratrace metals removed.
curation_added: citric acid (~9.4 mM, the principal milk organic acid — was absent)
orotic acid (~70 mg/L characteristic bovine pyrimidine — was absent)
quantitative lactose as the dominant sugar (~4.8 g/100g, 144.7 mM)
free glucose and free galactose amounts
full amino-acid profile with g/100g amounts
full mineral amounts incl. selenium, iodine, chloride

curation_removed.name: Arsenate
curation_removed.reason: toxic trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Cadmium
curation_removed.reason: toxic trace metal contaminant, not a nutrient

curation_removed.name: Rubidium
curation_removed.reason: non-nutritive trace element, not characteristic of milk

curation_removed.name: Boron
curation_removed.reason: non-nutritive ultratrace element, not a defined milk nutrient

curation_removed.name: Chromium
curation_removed.reason: ultratrace, not a defined milk growth nutrient (FooDB noise)

curation_removed.name: Fluoride
curation_removed.reason: ultratrace contaminant, not a growth nutrient of milk

curation_removed.name: Sucrose C12H22O11
curation_removed.reason: not a milk sugar; milk carbohydrate is lactose — FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: Maltose C12H22O11
curation_removed.reason: not a milk sugar; FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: D-Fructose
curation_removed.reason: not a characteristic milk sugar; FooDB noise (milk free sugars are glucose/galactose, not fructose)

curation_removed.name: D-Galactarate
curation_removed.reason: mucic acid — biologically implausible in fresh milk; FooDB predicted

curation_removed.name: Acetone
curation_removed.reason: trace volatile ketone (ketosis marker), not a nutrient

curation_removed.name: Methanol
curation_removed.reason: trace volatile, not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: 3-Methylbutanoic acid
curation_removed.reason: trace volatile flavor compound, not a quantitatively relevant nutrient

curation_removed.name: L Kynurenine C10H12N2O3
curation_removed.reason: trace tryptophan-catabolite metabolite, not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: N-acetyl-L-cysteine
curation_removed.reason: trace/predicted metabolite, not a defined milk nutrient

components.name: lactose
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC SR Legacy 'Milk, reduced fat, 2% milkfat, +vit A +vit D' (NDB 01079); total carbohydrate in fluid milk is essentially all lactose

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence_source: FooDB FOOD00891; Fox & McSweeney, Dairy Chemistry (free glucose ~0.07-0.1 g/L)

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence_source: FooDB FOOD00891; Fox & McSweeney, Dairy Chemistry

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence_source: Garnsworthy/CABI 'Variability of citric acid in cow milk' doi:10.5555/20123239825; classic bovine milk citrate 8-10 mM (~1.75 g/L)

components.name: L-lactate
components.evidence_source: Fox & McSweeney, Dairy Chemistry; fresh pasteurized milk 0-0.4 g/L

components.name: urea
components.evidence_source: FooDB FOOD00891; Dairy Chemistry (milk urea-N ~10-15 mg/dL)

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC SR Legacy NDB 01079 (8 mg/100g)

components.name: phosphatidylcholine
components.evidence_source: FooDB FOOD00891; milk phospholipid ~0.2-0.4 g/L, PC ~30% of MFGM PL

components.name: Chloride
components.evidence_source: FooDB FOOD00891; Dairy Chemistry (milk chloride ~1 g/L)

components.name: Iodine
components.evidence_source: McCance & Widdowson; UK/EU dairy iodine ~10-20 ug/100g (not in USDA panel)

components.name: Myo-inositol
components.evidence_source: FooDB FOOD00891; Dairy Chemistry (~0.11 g/L free inositol)
